author: Live A Quiet Life
description: A card-based note-taking system for research and study. Capture ideas and reading notes on virtual index cards, organized into projects and categories. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Live A Quiet Life
manifest.description: A card-based note-taking system for research and study. Capture ideas and reading notes on virtual index cards, organized into projects and categories.
manifest.name: Index Cards

name: Index Cards

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
- **Quick capture from any note.** Select text in a note, press a hotkey, and
  it becomes a new card in your last-active project — no need to open the
  plugin first. The card's body includes a `[[wikilink]]` back to the source
  note, so provenance is preserved and survives export as a live backlink.
  - When no project is currently active (e.g. after opening the plugin fresh),
    a project picker appears so you can choose a destination. Your choice
    becomes the new default for the next capture.
  - Bind the hotkey under **Settings > Hotkeys > "Quick capture selection to
    new card."**
- **Automatic rotating backups.** On startup, the plugin snapshots your card
  data file and keeps the five most recent copies. Backups are stored alongside
  the data file (not inside the plugin folder), so they survive uninstalling and
  reinstalling the plugin. A backup is skipped if the current data file cannot be
  read as valid JSON, preserving your last known-good copies.

### Improved

- **Search now covers citation fields.** Searching within a project now matches
  author, title, source/journal, publisher, place, year, and URL — not just
  card title, note text, and tags. Looking up every card by an author's surname
  or a publisher now works as expected.
- **Citation parser handles editor and translator credits.** Footnote-style
  citations that name editors, translators, or compilers (e.g. `eds.`, `ed.`,
  `trans.`, `comp.`) now attach that role to the author field instead of
  leaking it into the title. For example, *"Adele Berlin, Marc Zvi Brettler, and
  Michael Fishbane, eds., The Jewish Study Bible …"* now parses with the title
  as *The Jewish Study Bible* and the editors correctly credited — and renders
  properly in generated bibliographies.

### Removed

- Removed an unused internal citation-parsing function that was never called and
  carried a misleading comment. No user-facing behavior changes; the active
  clipboard citation parser is unaffected.

### Known limitations

- APA formatting of multi-author works still lists only the first author
  (pre-existing behavior; targeted for a future release).

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
## [1.1.1] - 2026-07-01

### Fixed

- **Citation parser: multi-word titles no longer misread as co-authors.** In the
  footnote fast-path, a title made of comma-separated single words (e.g.
  *Amos, Jonah, & Micah*) was being absorbed into the Author(s) field, pushing
  the real title and series into the wrong slots. Continuation author segments
  now must look like a personal name — containing a space (First Last) or a
  period (an initial) — so single-word title fragments fall through to the title
  correctly. The first author may still be a mononym (Augustine, Josephus).
  This path feeds Export to Outline, so affected footnotes now round-trip cleanly.

### Changed

- Removed an unused CSS rule (`.ic-card-hovered`) flagged by the community
  linter. The class was never applied — card hover is handled elsewhere — so
  there is no visual change. Drops two `!important` declarations.

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
### What's new in 1.1.0

- Added Export to Outline — generate a structured outline from your cards directly from the sandbox
- Fixed series vs. journal citation discriminator across all 5 citation styles
- Fixed comma-truncation bug in bibliography parser for titled book sections

releases.name: 1.0.5 - Fix duplicate entries in bibliography export

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.name: v1.0.4 - Kindle citation support, additional bug fixes

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
- Fixed console error in Compare Cards (MarkdownRenderer component)
- Fixed F key / focus not working when switching from another tab
- Fixed citation parser: "Claude" and similar single-word web titles
- Fixed citation parser: edited volume variants (APA, Chicago, MLA, SBL)
- Fixed citation parser: web citations (URL, Accessed date, site name)
- Fixed subcategory buttons too close to screen edge
releases.name: 1.0.3 - bug fixes and citation parser improvements
